# Baseline itinerary: 2-day Tokyo Food & Culture Stopover

## Goal and constraints understood
- **Origin:** Not specified (arrival assumed near Tokyo Station)
- **Dates:** 2026-04-18 to 2026-04-19
- **Budget:** 90,000 JPY for 2 travelers
- **Interests:** markets, temples, neighborhood walking, casual restaurants
- **Hotel area:** near Tokyo Station or Ginza

## Search summary
- Tsukiji Outer Market: confirmed open in 2026 with 460+ shops and food stalls
- Senso-ji Temple: open 6:00 AM–5:00 PM (April), free admission
- Ginza hotels: multiple options near Ginza Subway Station (Solaria Nishitetsu, Tokyu Stay, Millennium Mitsui Garden)
- Ginza walking attractions: Dover Street Market, Ginza Clock Tower, Art Aquarium Museum

## Day 1 – April 18 (Saturday)

| Time | Type | Plan | Location | Cost | Evidence/Notes |
|---|---|---|---|---:|---|
| 06:00-08:00 | breakfast | Tsukiji Outer Market food stalls | Tsukiji, Chuo | ¥3,000 | [Tanuki Tabi 2026 Guide](https://tanuki-tabi-travel.com/blog/tsukiji-market-guide) – 460+ stalls with diverse street food |
| 08:00-09:30 | walk | Walk from Tsukiji to Ginza | Tsukiji → Ginza | ¥0 | ~15-min walk; explore transition from market to luxury district |
| 09:30-11:30 | sightseeing | Ginza neighborhood walking tour | Ginza | ¥0 | [Nobu Tokyo Guide](https://nobu-tokyo.com/ginza-walking-tour-must-see-20-spots/) – 20 must-see spots including Dover Street Market, Ginza Clock Tower |
| 11:30-13:00 | lunch | Casual restaurant in Ginza | Ginza | ¥2,500 | Estimate; many casual eateries in the district |
| 13:00-15:00 | shopping/exploration | Ginza shopping streets | Ginza | ¥0 | Window shopping at luxury boutiques; explore side alleys |
| 15:00-17:00 | rest | Return to hotel check-in / rest | Hotel area | ¥0 | Assume hotel near Ginza Station |
| 17:30-19:30 | dinner | Casual dining in Ginza | Ginza | ¥3,500 | Estimate; yakitori or izakaya option |
| 19:30-21:00 | evening | Ginza illuminated streets walk | Ginza | ¥0 | Evening atmosphere with illuminated windows |

## Day 2 – April 19 (Sunday)

| Time | Type | Plan | Location | Cost | Evidence/Notes |
|---|---|---|---|---:|---|
| 06:00-07:30 | transport | Travel to Senso-ji Temple | Asakusa (via Ginza/Asakusa line) | ¥300 | Estimate; ~20 min from Ginza |
| 07:30-09:30 | temple visit | Senso-ji Temple (early morning) | Asakusa | ¥0 | [Trip.com](https://www.trip.com/travel-guide/attraction/tokyo/sens-ji-78892/) – Open 6AM-5PM, free admission; Tokyo's oldest temple (628 AD) |
| 09:30-11:00 | shopping | Nakamise Shopping Street | Asakusa | ¥1,500 | Souvenirs, snacks, traditional goods |
| 11:00-12:30 | lunch | Casual restaurant in Asakusa | Asakusa | ¥2,000 | Estimate; soba or tonkatsu options |
| 12:30-14:30 | exploration | Asakusa neighborhood walk | Asakusa | ¥0 | Thunder Gate (Kaminarimon), Sumida River views |
| 14:30-16:00 | transport | Return to Ginza/Tokyo Station area | Ginza | ¥300 | Estimate |
| 16:00-18:00 | free time | Final shopping or exploration | Ginza/Tokyo Station | ¥0 | Last-minute purchases |
| 18:00-20:00 | dinner | Farewell dinner | Near hotel | ¥3,500 | Estimate |
| 20:00-22:00 | rest/prepare | Return to hotel, rest | Hotel area | ¥0 | Early start on Day 1 |

## Budget summary

| Category | Estimated Cost (JPY) |
|---|---:|
| Hotel (2 nights, double room) | ¥50,000 |
| Food & drinks (2 days) | ¥14,500 |
| Transport (local) | ¥1,200 |
| Shopping/souvenirs | ¥5,000 |
| Contingency | ¥5,000 |
| **Total** | **¥75,700** |

**Remaining buffer:** ~¥14,300 (under budget)

## Unscheduled or uncertain items
- Specific hotel booking not confirmed – prices vary by availability; estimates based on mid-range Ginza options
- Flight arrival/departure details not provided
- Exact restaurant reservations not made; walk-in assumed for casual dining

## Assumptions and gaps
- Hotel prices assume mid-range business hotel near Ginza Station (~¥25,000/night); luxury options like The Tokyo EDITION or Conrad would exceed budget
- Tsukiji Outer Market best visited early morning (some stalls open 4:30 AM); timing adjusted accordingly
- Senso-ji Temple opens 6:00 AM in April – early visit avoids crowds
- Transport costs assume IC card (Suica/Pasmo) usage; exact routes not verified with live schedules
- No guided tours booked; self-guided walking based on public attractions
